agent-readiness.dnsAid.label: Verify your domain for AI
agent-readiness.dnsAid.description: Proves your business owns this site so agents can safely trust your content.
agent-readiness.linkHeaders.label: Guide AI bots to site data
agent-readiness.linkHeaders.description: Tells AI bots exactly where your structured product info and catalog are located.
agent-readiness.sitemap.label: Give agents a website map
agent-readiness.sitemap.description: Provides a clear directory layout so agents don't miss new pages or products.
agent-readiness.robotsTxt.label: Crawler Access Rules (robots.txt)
agent-readiness.robotsTxt.description: Controls which specific crawlers are allowed to read your website.
agent-readiness.markdownNegotiation.desc: Delivers clean, formatting-stripped text directly to agents instead of forcing them to scrape dense, complex webpage HTML layouts.
agent-readiness.contentSignals.label: Define how AI bots use your content
agent-readiness.contentSignals.descripti: Specifies how exactly your content can be used after accessed by AI bots.
agent-readiness.webBotAuth.label: Verify your site's outbound bots
agent-readiness.webBotAuth.description: Activates secure cryptographic signatures so other platforms can trust and verify when your own AI agents are making requests.
agent-readiness.robotsTxtAiRules.label: Manage specific AI bots
agent-readiness.robotsTxtAiRules.descrip: Sets individual access permissions for AI crawlers like OpenAI's GPTBot and Anthropic's Claude-Web to control what they can read.
agent-readiness.a2aAgentCard.label: Introduce your site's AI to other agents
agent-readiness.a2aAgentCard.description: Publishes a machine-readable profile card so external AI agents can discover and communicate directly with your site's AI.
agent-readiness.agentSkills.label: List what your AI agent can do
agent-readiness.agentSkills.description: Provides a structured catalog of the specific tasks, tools, and custom capabilities your native AI agent is authorized to run.
agent-readiness.apiCatalog.label: Expose a directory of your web services
agent-readiness.apiCatalog.description: Publishes an automated index so external AI tools can cleanly identify and query your data feeds without human guidance.
agent-readiness.authMd.label: Share login instructions for AI bots
agent-readiness.authMd.description: A standardized markdown file that tells AI bots exactly how to securely register and authenticate with your platform.
agent-readiness.mcpServerCard.label: Share your AI tool context engine
agent-readiness.mcpServerCard.descriptio: Exposes an endpoint using the Model Context Protocol (MCP), allowing external LLMs to securely tap into your site's secure tools and prompts.
agent-readiness.oauthDiscovery.label: Automate security handshakes for bots
agent-readiness.oauthDiscovery.descripti: Provides a public configuration file so visiting AI agents know exactly where and how to verify their login keys.
agent-readiness.oauthProtectedResource.d: Explicitly tells visiting agents what security permissions they must pass to view premium or private data areas.
agent-readiness.webMcp.label: Let AI agents run in-browser tools
agent-readiness.webMcp.description: Safely bridges browser-level workflows to an AI agent's reasoning loop so it can interact smoothly with your web app interfaces.
agent-readiness.acp.label: Connect AI bots to your product catalog
agent-readiness.acp.description: Deploys commerce discovery metadata so shopping bots can automatically scan your inventory, pricing, and active listings.
agent-readiness.ap2.label: Enable secure AI checkout loops
agent-readiness.ap2.description: Adopts the Agent Payments Protocol, giving AI agents the ability to complete transactions on behalf of their users.
agent-readiness.mpp.label: Accept automated machine payments
agent-readiness.mpp.description: Integrates the Machine Payment Protocol to seamlessly clear instant, bot-native HTTP checkouts on your web server.
agent-readiness.ucp.label: Handle paywalls for AI content consumption
agent-readiness.ucp.description: Uses the Universal Commerce Protocol to clear friction when AI agents need to pay for accessing your premium articles or database feeds.
agent-readiness.x402.label: Process micro-payments for bot API calls
agent-readiness.x402.description: Standardizes automated HTTP 402 rules so bots can instantly settle tiny micro-transactions per request or per query.
